subclauses.title: Understanding the organisation and its context

questions.question: Has the organisation determined internal issues that can affect the ISMS (e.g. culture, capabilities, processes)?
Has the organisation determined external issues that can affect the ISMS (e.g. legal, technological, market, partner ecosystem)?
Is the context review performed at planned intervals and when material changes occur?

subclauses.title: Understanding the needs and expectations of interested parties

questions.question: Are relevant interested parties (e.g. customers, regulators, parent company) identified for the ISMS scope?
Are applicable requirements and expectations of those parties (including which are mandatory) recorded?

subclauses.title: Determining the scope of the information security management system

questions.question: Is the ISMS scope defined with boundaries and applicability, linked to the organisation’s context and services?
Is the scope available as documented information and suitable for certification purposes?
Is the ISMS established, implemented, maintained, and improved using the PDCA approach required by the standard?
Are processes and their interactions (inputs/outputs) defined for the ISMS, including for outsourced work?
Is top management accountable for the effectiveness of the ISMS and ensuring policy and objectives align to strategy?
Is resources assignment, risk ownership, and continual improvement direction demonstrated by management?
Does management support integration of ISMS requirements into business processes and promote awareness of importance?
Is an information security policy established, appropriate to the organisation, and approved by top management?
Are commitments to satisfy applicable requirements and continual improvement included in the policy?
Is the policy communicated, available, and (as relevant) acknowledged by interested parties?
Are ISMS roles, responsibilities, and authorities assigned and documented?
Is there an appointed information security function or equivalent with clear mandate?

subclauses.title: Actions to address risks and opportunities

questions.question: Is the information security risk assessment process defined, including criteria, methods, and ownership of outcomes?
Is the risk treatment plan integrated with the management system and resourced to achieve objectives?
Are opportunities to improve the ISMS considered alongside treatment of information security risks?
Is the link between information security risk results and the statement of applicable controls and objectives explicit and up to date?

subclauses.title: Information security objectives and planning to achieve them

questions.question: Are measurable information security objectives established at relevant functions/levels, consistent with the policy?
Is planning documented for what will be done, resources, responsibilities, timeframes, and how results are evaluated?
How is objective achievement measured and is progress reported to management in a way that supports decisions?
When the ISMS needs change, is the change carried out in a planned manner, considering risk impacts?
Describe a recent change to the ISMS and how information security was considered in planning.
Are change requests linked to the risk assessment and/or management review when they affect scope, assets, or controls?
Are necessary resources (people, time, money, technology) made available to establish and maintain the ISMS?
Is budget/forecast evidence available showing investment aligned to information security needs?
For persons whose work affects information security, is necessary competence defined and how attainment is determined?
Provide training records, certifications, or gap analyses supporting competence claims for key ISMS roles.
Do relevant personnel understand the ISMS policy, their contribution, and consequences of nonconformity?
Is security awareness content relevant to job role and provided at defined intervals, with metrics where appropriate?
What, when, with whom, and how the organisation will communicate on ISMS matters, including external parties?
Are communications (internal and external) recorded where needed for the ISMS?
Is the extent of documented information determined for the ISMS, including where it is created, updated, and controlled?
Are distribution, access, storage, and retention/ disposition controls applied to protect documented information appropriately?
Are information security plans implemented and controls operated as part of the risk treatment plan?
Are changes to planned activities assessed for their impact on the ISMS and controlled (including outsourced work)?
Is control implementation aligned to documented plans and are exceptions tracked?
Are risk assessments performed at planned intervals and on significant change?
Is risk information retained, including how likelihood and impact were derived for significant risks?
Is treatment consistency with methodology reviewed when business context, assets, or threats change meaningfully?
Is a statement of applicable controls (incl. justification for Annex A) maintained and is residual risk owner-approved where needed?
Show evidence of implementation status for the statement of applicable controls, including for suppliers.
If controls are not implemented, is risk treatment decision-making recorded (e.g. risk acceptance) with sign-off?
Are what needs to be monitored and measured, methods, and evaluation criteria for ISMS performance defined?
Are results of monitoring reviewed by appropriate personnel and is evidence of trends retained (e.g. dashboards, reports)?
Is performance data used to support management review and opportunities for improvement?
Is an internal audit programme planned with defined frequency, scope, and criteria, independent to the degree practicable?
Can you provide recent internal audit reports, findings, and evidence of follow-up/closure of nonconformities?
Are auditors competent and do audit records demonstrate ISO 27001 (ISMS) requirements were covered in scope?
Does top management review the ISMS at planned intervals, including inputs (audit, incidents, risk changes) and output decisions (resources, changes)?
Are management review minutes/records of decisions retained and is follow-through visible?
Describe how the outcome of the last review changed priorities or the ISMS within the last cycle.
Does the organisation actively pursue continual improvement of the suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness of the ISMS?
Is improvement data sourced from performance evaluation, lessons learned, and objectives progress?
Are security incidents, near misses, and trends used as inputs to improve controls and the ISMS?
Is there a process to prioritise, assign ownership, and track improvement actions with measurable completion criteria?
When a nonconformity occurs, is the reaction, cause analysis, and corrective action (including need for change) taken?
Provide a corrective action record showing root cause, action plan, review of effectiveness, and updated risks where relevant.
Are nonconformities (including from audits) tracked to closure and is recurrence prevented to the extent practicable?
Are corrective actions completed within target timescales, with re-testing or verification of controls where required?
